title: Is my estrogen too high?

⚕️ Always consult a licensed healthcare professional before changing your protocol.","contextSession":{"id":"d8e3cfa3-4357-4e7b-aea1-b8756cbf6be6","testName":"Blood Test","testDate":"2026-01-09"},"timestamp":"2026-04-14T09:25:30.677Z"},{"id":"0.c6goh16kw4h","role":"user","content":"if i was to start trt what dose would you recommend","timestamp":"2026-04-14T09:25:56.617Z"},{"id":"0.ycxik5o4z4","role":"assistant","content":"I cannot recommend a specific **TRT** dosage, as that requires a clinical assessment by a healthcare professional considering your symptoms, goals, and comprehensive health history. However, with your **Testosterone** at **9 nmol/L** (ref: **12–30 nmol/L**) and your **SHBG** at **17.4 nmol/L** (ref: **10–57 nmol/L**), **TRT** is a strong consideration. Typically, starting protocols often involve testosterone esters (like cypionate or enanthate) dosed weekly or twice weekly, with the aim of achieving total testosterone levels in the upper physiological range (e.g., **20-30 nmol/L**). Your low **SHBG** means you might require slightly less frequent injections to maintain stable levels, but this needs to be dialled in with follow-up blood work to monitor levels and manage **oestradiol** if necessary.

⚕️ Always consult a licensed healthcare professional before changing your protocol.","contextSession":{"id":"d8e3cfa3-4357-4e7b-aea1-b8756cbf6be6","testName":"Blood Test","testDate":"2026-01-09"},"timestamp":"2026-04-14T09:26:00.488Z"},{"id":"0.lotu5uhpf4","role":"user","content":"would 100mg a week be okay","timestamp":"2026-04-14T09:26:37.647Z"},{"id":"0.qbc555angk","role":"assistant","content":"A dose of **100mg/week** of testosterone is a common starting point for **TRT** and often aims to bring total **Testosterone** into the mid-to-upper physiological range for many individuals. title: What would happen if I introduce HGH?
messages_json: [{"id":"0.8dxcg1k513u","role":"user","content":"What would happen if I introduce HGH?","timestamp":"2026-04-14T16:22:39.142Z"},{"id":"0.po0ufp1ksl","role":"assistant","content":"Introducing HGH with your current panel, particularly your high **Testosterone** (**33.9 nmol/L** ⚠ OUT OF RANGE) and borderline **SHBG** (**53.2 nmol/L** ⚡ BORDERLINE), will likely lower SHBG, increasing your free testosterone significantly. This could elevate estrogenic side effects, so monitoring your E2 is crucial. Also, HGH can affect insulin sensitivity, so keep a close eye on your **HbA1c** (**32.46 mmol/mol**), and be aware of potential impacts on your borderline lipid markers (HDL, LDL, Total Cholesterol).\n⚕️ Always consult a licensed healthcare professional before changing your protocol.","contextSession":{"id":"c2a0317c-8c99-45cc-8861-37b8db74f88a","testName":"December 2025 Test","testDate":"2026-04-14"},"timestamp":"2026-04-14T16:22:46.351Z"}]

title: Are my liver markers OK?
